display_name: The Master Smith of the Iron Pillar
one_line: Anonymous Gupta-era master ironsmith who forges the rust-defying Vishnudhvaja for King Chandra.

appearance.age_look: Early 30s, lean and wiry with a blacksmith’s muscular forearms and solid shoulders.
appearance.face: Oval face with high cheekbones, medium-brown North Indian complexion, straight nose, faint burn scar along the left jawline, and calm, contemplative eyes with a default expression of focused determination and quiet pride.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair oiled and pulled into a short, neat topknot at the crown (shikha-like) with the sides and back cut short; a plain dark-brown cloth headband tied around the forehead to catch sweat, knot resting at the back.
appearance.costume: Gupta-period artisan attire: a knee-length, off-white cotton dhoti wrapped tightly around the waist and pulled between the legs in a practical working style, secured with a narrow dark-red cotton waist sash; bare upper body except for a simple dark-brown leather apron front panel hanging from a leather strap over the shoulders, reaching mid-thigh and showing soot and burn marks; a single thin copper armlet on the right upper arm and a black cotton thread (kalava-style) around the right wrist; simple brown leather sandals with a thong between the toes; a small cloth pouch in dark indigo tied at the back of the sash, hanging over his right hip.
appearance.signature: A heavy, slightly soot-blackened wrought-iron sledgehammer about chest-high, with a thick square head and a straight dark-wood handle wrapped in tan leather near the grip, carried in his right hand or resting on his shoulder.

ref_desc: A lean Gupta-era ironsmith in an off-white dhoti with dark-red sash, bare torso under a soot-stained dark-brown leather apron, black hair in a short topknot with a brown headband, iron-grey sledgehammer in hand, brown sandals, and accents of indigo cloth.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a Gupta-era Indian master ironsmith: an early-30s, lean but muscular man with medium-brown complexion, oval face, high cheekbones, calm determined eyes, and a faint burn scar along his left jawline. His black hair is oiled and pulled into a short topknot at the crown, with the sides and back cut short, and a plain dark-brown cloth headband tied around his forehead, knot at the back. He wears a knee-length off-white cotton dhoti tightly wrapped and drawn between the legs for work, secured with a narrow dark-red cotton waist sash, and a simple dark-brown leather apron front panel hanging from a shoulder strap over his bare torso, the apron reaching mid-thigh and visibly marked with soot and burn spots. Add a thin copper armlet on his right upper arm, a black thread bracelet on his right wrist, and a small dark indigo cloth pouch tied at the back of his sash and hanging over his right hip. On his feet are simple brown leather sandals. His signature weapon is a chest-high, iron-grey wrought-iron sledgehammer with a thick square head and a straight dark-wood handle wrapped in tan leather near the grip, which he holds in his right hand or rests on his shoulder. Use a grounded, earthy palette dominated by off-white, dark red, dark brown, indigo, and iron grey, and show him from front and three-quarter views to clearly display face, hair, clothing, and the hammer.
